If I take a step back, post-World War 2, both Germany and Japan's youth and the entire nation worked hard to rebuild their nation. Some such sense needs to be imbibed right at the school stage in Nepal so as to bring nation-building pride and economic contentment. It is not a very long haul, but rightly done, we can perhaps see positive results in just over a decade. Just blaming the government is an excuse. But sadly to majority of Nepali youths this sounds like poetry. Social media and peer pressure is spoiling our young minds right at the school level leaving an already jeopardized nation to darker abyss. Our boys and girls want to settle the rest of their lives in the best nations (Mostly just following the trend and FOMO). Economic reason is not universal in all migrations.

BUT BEWARE. REVERSE MIGRATION is happening. The developed host nations like Canada, US, Australia, Japan are already getting overwhelmed with migrants from poor countries. Lack of housing, rising food prices and electricity bills, racism, safety, long process for healthcare facilities and lack of jobs are just the struggles that has to be kept in mind. People from some poor countries are returning back to their nations citing above mentioned reasons. That is going to increase more. ANTI IMMIGRATION SENTIMENTS IS ON THE RISE. They are fed up of brown/black/asian skins. It’s a truth even if they don’t express. Migrant baseko area ma gora haru mostly basdainan.
But at the end it is all Poetry. 😅. Des banauney kam ma ko lagcha? Kehi faida chaina. Netaharuley bananuna parney ho des. Hamro kam ho ra? Bidesko chillo bato, aglo building, hariyo park and sea beach samjhiyepachi control nai hudaina. Jasari bhayepani gayincha hai?

Yes, the issue of getting equivalence certificate from Tribhuvan University is real. This is causing many to rethink about returning to Nepal or to leave Nepal after becoming frustrated with the TU people here.  I know folks who have masters and PhD degrees from western countries. They struggle to get equivalence at TU because TU doesn't have a comparable degree program or study route as found in western universities. If you study a field not found at TU, getting equivalence is very difficult. Also, TU may not give equivalence for masters degree if the field of study is different than the bachelor's degree. Same goes with PhD. They make a big issue on how one can go from one degree to another, even if it was completely legal overseas. If equivalence is not granted for masters, then it will not be granted for PhD. Even those who study in India are at risk of not getting equivalence. Hence, you have cases where people can get equivalent certificates from other foreign countries but not from Nepal! For example, an America-educated person can get equivalent certificates from UK and Canada for the purpose of employment in those countries, but not from Nepal! It is very sad indeed!
